extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property (never rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true) is VIOLATED. rec_drug_b becomes true only via r1 (IF hepatic_impairment, priority 50). rec_drug_a becomes true only via r4 (IF hepatic_impairment AND hyperkalemia AND low_ejection_fraction AND pregnancy AND elderly, priority 50). Critically, r4's condition requires hepatic_impairment as a conjunct, so any input that fires r4 necessarily also satisfies r1's condition. No rule ever sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false, and the default only applies to outputs left unset, so neither true value is overridden. Therefore the input hepatic_impairment=true, hyperkalemia=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, pregnancy=true, elderly=true fires both r1 (rec_drug_b:=true) and r4 (rec_drug_a:=true), yielding rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously. bradycardia and heart_failure only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant (set to false here for a complete minimal assignment).
